content.full_text:
tate olCali 
air Political Practices Commissiort 
SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
r·1arch 14, 1977 
~r. Ralph Juvinall 
Republican Consultants 
California State Assembly 
State Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Juvinall: 
Re: 
Opinion Request 
No. 75-01d-B 
On January 21, 1975, you asked the Fair Political 
Practices Commission for a formal opinion interpreting the mass 
mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
Subsequently, 
your opinion request was consolidated with those received from 
several other California officials. 
On August 3, 1976, the 
Commission adopted an opinion, 2 FPPC Ops. 110, in which it 
answered all of the questions posed by the various requests, 
except those concerning the applicability of the mass mailing 
provisions of Government Code Section 84305 to certain mailings 
made on behalf of legislators at public expense. 
Although the Commission has indicated that certain 
types of government expenditures are reportable under the Polit-
ical Reform Act as contributions and/or expenditures, precise 
guidelines outlining reporting obligations in the context of 
publicly financed mass mailings, and hence the applicability of 
Government Code Section 84305 to such mailings, have not yet 
been promulgated. 
However, the staff presently is researching 
and drafting regulations that will establish the necessary 
guidelines, and these regulations will be responsive to the 
question posed in your opinion request. 
Because the r2gulations 
will address the issue posed in your opinion request, the Com-
Qission decided, at its meeting on March 2, 1977, that a formal 
opinion would not be necessary and that the remaining portion 
of your opinion request should be dismissed. 
Accordingly, no 
formal opinion will be issued in response to t 
remaining 
question in your request. 
When drafts of the proposed regulations are avail 
e, 
co~ies will be provided to you for your comQents and suggestions. 
Any comments should be directed to Tee Prim, an attorney on the 
Commission staff. 


Mr. Ralph Juvinall 
!VIa r c h 14, 1977 
Page Two 
I apologize for the long delay in answering your 
opinion request. 
If you have any further questions, please 
feel free to contact me. 
11 ichael 3ennet t 
~xecutive Director

sections.question_synthetic: Whether government expenditures for mass mailings made on behalf of legislators are reportable as contributions/expenditures under the Political Reform Act and subject to Government Code Section 84305.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to issue a formal opinion, instead indicating that staff is researching and drafting regulations to establish guidelines for reporting obligations in publicly financed mass mailings, and that these forthcoming regulations will address the question posed.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a declination letter, not a traditional advice letter with formal question and conclusion sections. The document references a consolidated opinion request originally filed January 21, 1975, with a prior opinion issued August 3, 1976. The letter dismisses the remaining unanswered questions pending regulatory development.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to issue a formal opinion on questions regarding the applicability of mass mailing provisions to publicly financed legislative mailings, noting that staff is developing regulations that will address the issue and that a formal opinion is therefore unnecessary.
